source_refs.rationale: Taxat actor classes split client, tenant-human, service, and external boundaries before authorization is evaluated.
The IdP may bootstrap authentication posture, but the engine still owns the final ALLOW or REQUIRE_STEP_UP or REQUIRE_APPROVAL or DENY decision.
Browser, native, machine, and limited-entry session channels must remain distinct and challenge rotation must invalidate stale pre-step-up state.
Current Auth0 RBAC guidance allows coarse role and permission assignment while keeping richer authorization in the application.
Current Auth0 step-up guidance relies on requested scopes and Actions rather than a single tenant-wide MFA toggle.
Current Auth0 session guidance supports tenant defaults plus per-login overrides through Actions.

scopes.label: Portal read
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Allows the client portal shell to open after login; it does not prove delegation to any specific reporting subject.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still decides client scope, masking, and per-object legality.

source_refs.rationale: Portal viewer-style access is a coarse client-facing posture.

scopes.label: Portal contribute
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Allows onboarding and upload-capable entry posture once the normal authenticated portal session exists.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still validates upload-session binding, request scope, and stale-view guards.

source_refs.rationale: Upload and onboarding commands are typed backend commands and remain server-authorized.

scopes.label: Portal signatory base
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Marks that the account may enter signatory-capable portal surfaces before pack-specific legality is checked.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still decides if a particular approval pack is signable and whether fresh step-up proof is mandatory.

source_refs.rationale: CLIENT_SIGNATORY is distinct from viewer or contributor and may later need STEP_UP_VERIFIED assurance.

scopes.label: Operator workspace
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Allows the internal operator shell to open after sign-in.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: Per-client delegation, authority-link safety, and command legality remain server-authored.

source_refs.rationale: Operator and governance surfaces are shared shell vocabulary, but action legality is not string-matched from role names.

scopes.label: Governance read
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Allows access to governance-density routes and policy explainability surfaces.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: Mutation authority still depends on structured AUTHORIZE(...) results, not this coarse scope alone.

source_refs.rationale: Governance views must show separate permission, delegation, authority-link, and authn dimensions.

scopes.label: Audit read masked
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Allows masked audit and provenance inspection as a baseline read posture.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: Unmasked export still requires an elevated request plus server-side legality.

source_refs.rationale: ALLOW_MASKED is a first-class decision that must stay explicit.

scopes.label: Support workspace
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Allows support-oriented investigation surfaces without implying governance admin posture.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still decides if the support operator may annotate or export unmasked evidence.

source_refs.rationale: Support and audit investigation are explicit governed surfaces with their own viewer capability profiles.

scopes.label: Machine runtime
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Allows service-to-service machine identity for product runtime clients only.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still checks per-command PrincipalContext, idempotency, and service scope.

source_refs.rationale: Service principals stay first-class actors but never substitute for humans.

scopes.label: IdP bootstrap manage
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Allows the dedicated provider bootstrap client to manage Auth0-compatible control-plane settings.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: This scope is outside product-runtime authorization and remains limited to provisioning automation.

source_refs.rationale: The earlier IdP topology card established a separate provider-management bootstrap client family.

scopes.label: Elevated authority link
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Requesting this scope is the coarse signal that an authority-link or relink operation needs fresh MFA-capable step-up.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still validates AuthorityBinding freshness, subject match, and bounded client scope.

source_refs.rationale: Linking or re-linking software to the authority is explicitly elevated.
Current Auth0 step-up guidance uses requested scopes plus Actions to trigger MFA for sensitive resources.

scopes.label: Elevated override approval
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Requests fresh MFA-capable posture before override approval affecting filing or parity outcome.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still validates rationale, expiry, exact scope, and stale-view basis.

source_refs.rationale: Override approval is explicitly elevated and bounded.

scopes.label: Elevated submit
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Requests fresh MFA-capable posture before operator-side submission or amendment send.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still validates approval state, trust, authority link, packet readiness, and send-time identity.

source_refs.rationale: Submission requires a current preflight sequence, not just a coarse elevated scope.

scopes.label: Elevated out-of-band annotation
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Requests fresh human-gated posture before an out-of-band truth annotation is staged.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still keeps UNKNOWN and OUT_OF_BAND truth distinct from confirmed authority acknowledgement.

source_refs.rationale: Out-of-band truth cannot silently become confirmed authority truth.

scopes.label: Elevated unmasked export
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Requests fresh MFA-capable posture before full evidence or unmasked provenance export.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still evaluates masking rules, export posture, retention gates, and admissibility.

source_refs.rationale: Unmasked export is explicitly elevated.

scopes.label: Elevated config approval
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Requests fresh MFA-capable posture before compliance-mode config approval.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still verifies dependency topology hash, simulation basis hash, and bounded_safe_mutation posture.

source_refs.rationale: Governance mutation-capable actions preserve extra hashes and approval requirements beyond IdP posture.

scopes.label: Elevated retention exception
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Requests fresh MFA-capable posture before erasure, legal-hold release, or retention exception execution.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still checks legal-hold, retention minimum, and irreversible mutation guards.

source_refs.rationale: Retention exceptions are explicitly elevated and bounded.

scopes.label: Elevated governance mutation
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Requests fresh MFA-capable posture for governance mutations that widen access, change step-up posture, or relink authority scope.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still binds the exact mutation tuple, affected client scope, and stale-view basis.

source_refs.rationale: Governance mutations that widen access or relink authority scope are elevated by source law.

scopes.label: Elevated client sign-off
scopes.idp_enforcement_meaning: Requests a fresh MFA-capable signatory checkpoint when the current approval pack demands step-up.
scopes.engine_authorization_boundary: The engine still checks approval-pack hash, stale-view guard, declaration acknowledgement, and signability.

source_refs.rationale: Sign-off requires fresh step-up proof whenever the pack or frozen policy demands it.
Current Auth0 step-up guidance supports requesting elevated scopes to trigger MFA for sensitive routes.

notes: Elevated scopes are request-time challenge hints only; the backend still owns the final command-level authorization result.
